speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Good Signior Angelo, you must excuse us all;

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: My wife is shrewish when I keep not hours.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Say that I lingered with you at your shop

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: To see the making of her carcanet,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And that tomorrow you will bring it home.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But here’s a villain that would face me down

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: He met me on the mart, and that I beat him

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And charged him with a thousand marks in gold,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And that I did deny my wife and house.—

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Thou drunkard, thou, what didst thou mean by this?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Say what you will, sir, but I know what I know.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That you beat me at the mart I have your hand to show;

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: If the skin were parchment and the blows you gave were ink,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Your own handwriting would tell you what I think.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I think thou art an ass.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Marry, so it doth appear

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: By the wrongs I suffer and the blows I bear.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I should kick being kicked and, being at that pass,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: You would keep from my heels and beware of an ass.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: You’re sad, Signior Balthasar. Pray God our cheer

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: May answer my goodwill and your good welcome here.

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: I hold your dainties cheap, sir, and your welcome dear.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: O Signior Balthasar, either at flesh or fish

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: A table full of welcome makes scarce one dainty dish.

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Good meat, sir, is common; that every churl affords.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And welcome more common, for that’s nothing but words.

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Small cheer and great welcome makes a merry feast.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Ay, to a niggardly host and more sparing guest.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But though my cates be mean, take them in good part.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Better cheer may you have, but not with better heart.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text:
But soft! My door is locked.
                 
                To
                 
                Dromio
                .
               Go, bid them let us in.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Maud, Bridget, Marian, Ciceley, Gillian, Ginn!

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Mome, malt-horse, capon, coxcomb, idiot, patch!

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Either get thee from the door or sit down at the hatch.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Dost thou conjure for wenches, that thou call’st for such store

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: When one is one too many? Go, get thee from the door.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: What patch is made our porter? My master stays in the street.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Let him walk from whence he came, lest he catch cold on ’s feet.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Who talks within there? Ho, open the door.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Right, sir, I’ll tell you when an you’ll tell me wherefore.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Wherefore? For my dinner. I have not dined today.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Nor today here you must not. Come again when you may.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: What art thou that keep’st me out from the house I owe?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: The porter for this time, sir, and my name is Dromio.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: O villain, thou hast stolen both mine office and my name!

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: The one ne’er got me credit, the other mickle blame.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: If thou hadst been Dromio today in my place,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Thou wouldst have changed thy face for a name, or thy name for an ass.

speaker: LUCE
text: What a coil is there, Dromio! Who are those at the gate?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Let my master in, Luce.

speaker: LUCE
text: Faith, no, he comes too late,

speaker: LUCE
text: And so tell your master.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: O Lord, I must laugh.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Have at you with a proverb: shall I set in my staff?

speaker: LUCE
text: Have at you with another: that’s—When, can you tell?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: If thy name be called “Luce,” Luce, thou hast answered him well.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Do you hear, you minion? You’ll let us in, I hope?

speaker: LUCE
text: I thought to have asked you.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And you said no.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: So, come help. Well struck! There was blow for blow.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Thou baggage, let me in.

speaker: LUCE
text: Can you tell for whose sake?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Master, knock the door hard.

speaker: LUCE
text: Let him knock till it ache.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: You’ll cry for this, minion, if I beat the door down.

speaker: LUCE
text: What needs all that, and a pair of stocks in the town?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Who is that at the door that keeps all this noise?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: By my troth, your town is troubled with unruly boys.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Are you there, wife? You might have come before.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Your wife, sir knave? Go, get you from the door.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: If you went in pain, master, this knave would go sore.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Here is neither cheer, sir, nor welcome. We would fain have either.

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: In debating which was best, we shall part with neither.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: They stand at the door, master. Bid them welcome hither.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: There is something in the wind, that we cannot get in.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: You would say so, master, if your garments were thin.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Your cake here is warm within; you stand here in the cold.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: It would make a man mad as a buck to be so bought and sold.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Go, fetch me something. I’ll break ope the gate.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Break any breaking here, and I’ll break your knave’s pate.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: A man may break a word with you, sir, and words are but wind,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Ay, and break it in your face, so he break it not behind.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: It seems thou want’st breaking. Out upon thee, hind!

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Here’s too much “Out upon thee!” I pray thee, let me in.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Ay, when fowls have no feathers and fish have no fin.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Well, I’ll break in. Go, borrow me a crow.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: A crow without feather? Master, mean you so?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: For a fish without a fin, there’s a fowl without a feather.—

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: If a crow help us in, sirrah, we’ll pluck a crow together.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Go, get thee gone. Fetch me an iron crow.

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Have patience, sir. O, let it not be so.

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Herein you war against your reputation,

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: And draw within the compass of suspect

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Th’ unviolated honor of your wife.

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Once this: your long experience of her wisdom,

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Her sober virtue, years, and modesty

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Plead on her part some cause to you unknown.

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: And doubt not, sir, but she will well excuse

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Why at this time the doors are made against you.

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Be ruled by me; depart in patience,

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: And let us to the Tiger all to dinner,

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: And about evening come yourself alone

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: To know the reason of this strange restraint.

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: If by strong hand you offer to break in

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Now in the stirring passage of the day,

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: A vulgar comment will be made of it;

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: And that supposèd by the common rout

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Against your yet ungallèd estimation

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: That may with foul intrusion enter in

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: And dwell upon your grave when you are dead;

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: For slander lives upon succession,

speaker: BALTHASAR
text: Forever housèd where it gets possession.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: You have prevailed. I will depart in quiet

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And, in despite of mirth, mean to be merry.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I know a wench of excellent discourse,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Pretty and witty, wild and yet, too, gentle.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: There will we dine. This woman that I mean,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: My wife—but, I protest, without desert—

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Hath oftentimes upbraided me withal;

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text:
To her will we to dinner.
                 
                To
                 
                Angelo
                .
               Get you home

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And fetch the chain; by this, I know, ’tis made.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Bring it, I pray you, to the Porpentine,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: For there’s the house. That chain will I bestow—

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Be it for nothing but to spite my wife—

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Upon mine hostess there. Good sir, make haste.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Since mine own doors refuse to entertain me,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I’ll knock elsewhere, to see if they’ll disdain me.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I’ll meet you at that place some hour hence.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Do so. This jest shall cost me some expense.
